summary: Extraction fidelity is accepted for this bounded safety-table repair. The proposed D+Q bone preferred-term rows match the retained PMC author-manuscript table and safety narrative, the registry discontinuation record stays tied to the retained ClinicalTrials.gov participant-flow source, and DKD, IPF, and AD-risk safety records remain limited to PubMed source-snapshot-backed abstract extraction.

findings.claim_or_issue: The proposed D+Q bone adverse-event preferred-term rows preserve the publication-table counts for diarrhea, nausea, constipation, gastrointestinal reflux, vertigo, fatigue, and body pain, with retained text locators to Extended Data Table 1 and the safety/adverse-events narrative.
findings.why_it_matters: Release-boundary acceptance should only include the new result rows if their counts and caveats are traceable to retained local source text.
findings.recommended_action: No revision required; keep the publication-table rows source-located and keep blank control cells encoded as not_reported_or_below_threshold rather than zero.

findings.claim_or_issue: The discontinuation result is explicitly registry/article descriptive, and the DKD, IPF, and AD-risk safety records do not claim full-text extraction where only PubMed source-snapshot abstract summaries are retained.
findings.why_it_matters: This prevents registry participant-flow reasons and abstract-level safety statements from being promoted as synthesis-ready full safety tables.
findings.recommended_action: No revision required; retain the maturity_status and provenance distinctions already present on the proposed result records.
